item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Slurry. If there ever was a word that pushed a skip over the edge, it's that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can accept this! +1 for being super fucking gross, good job :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the Soylent of body horror; all the nutrients in an easily consumed slurry. Once tried, always preferred.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that was a really good call. For some reason the long string of black boxes make whatever's lying underneath more visceral than just an expungement. I can't explain why but I really like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much seconding this. It feels like it's trying really hard to be gross as its crux, which is falling flat for me. There are some bits in here that I like but not enough to push me out of neutral territory.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, it works really well for me. In most cases I'm not a huge fan of redaction, but this particular use of it emphasises "something is going on behind these boxes that you really do not want to know about, related to the ingestion of slurry" and my mind is unfortunately brimming with possibilities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, it's basically a [DATA EXPUNGED]. I was feeling whimsical, so I went with the "lots of blackboxes" route instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The reason for the intense redaction of this passage is the only thing keeping me from an upvote at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't often see anomalies that rely a lot on the gross factor for their quality, so this caught me a bit by surprise. Not really what was expecting to read before breakfast.

Ingestion of the slurry produced by SCP-3269 should be studiously avoided by wearing appropriate safety gear at all times. ███-████ █████████ ████ ████ █████ ██ ███████ ███████ ██████████. ███ ██████ ███ ████ ████ ████████ ██ ██████████ █████████, █████ ██████ █ ██████████ ██ ███ ████████ ██████████ ██ █████ ██████ ██████ ██ ███-████.

Yummy. Just curious, but is there actual information behind these blackboxes? It looks like there might be.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this is the worst thing you've done so far.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was quite disgusting, but I couldn't stop reading. Here, take your +1, I'm going to go wash my brain now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: absolutely disgusting. one of those rare instances where i do NOT want to know what's behind the blackboxes. again, thank you for making this an image-less article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey i already said it in the chat but i hate this vehemently and it deserves an upvote but someone other than me is going to have to give it for me because i feel nauseous reading it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've never really understood the fascination with popping. As someone rarely bothered by body horror (with some notable exceptions), I found the article seemed to rely primarily on that and as a result, wasn't overwhelmed with what I assume was supposed to be the main focus. I also felt like the addendum seemed to restate a lot of information that should be considered obvious.

I'm really interested in what caused the update at the end, but it wasn't enough to tip me in the other direction. For now, no-vote.

item.title: 10pm on a sunday, you know what that means: BOLDPOSTING
item.content:encoded:
I felt like getting this out of my sandbox, so I polished it off and am now boldposting. I had a lot of trouble figuring out how to finish this, and I hope this works.

Thanks to the "popping" subreddit (NSFL); while they were not involved in the creation of this, they provided the direct inspiration and hours of entertainment.
